laws.title: Public Law 11-72 | No More Mariah Act (Keywords: Judges, Justices, Circuit Court)

provisions.evidence: This act shall be referred to as the No More Mariah Act.
Public Law 10-65, the Federal Judicial Reform Act II is repealed.
28 U.S. Code section 133 ... shall read as: (a) The President shall appoint ... up to five judges for the District of Columbia.
provisions.notes: This is the live Title 28 rewrite.

provisions.evidence: This act shall immediately take effect when the Federal Circuit Court of the District of Columbia is entirely vacated.

current_implementation.detail: The review record parks a boilerplate note at a Title 28 section 1 anchor, but the actual target is 28 U.S.C. 133 and the repeal of Public Law 10-65 must be preserved as history.

source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-011-072/law.txt contains the repeal of Public Law 10-65 and the replacement text for 28 U.S.C. section 133.
audit/primary/batch-12-02.json classifies the law as current and amendment-required.
audit/claude-validation.json agrees that the act is current and notes the Title 28 amendment gap.
Current XML grep did not recover a matching rp-pl011072 note in the branch or in origin/main.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch XML search did not recover a matching PL-011-072 note or a live 28 U.S.C. 133 rewrite, and origin/main does not show a better placement.
laws.final_recommended_action: Move the enactment into 28 U.S.C. 133, preserve the repeal of Public Law 10-65 as history, and remove boilerplate note material.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology supports the current branch treatment and the law remains aligned with the documented codification path.

laws.title: Public Law 12-74 | Federal Bar Association Act of 2023

provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Federal Bar Association Act of 2023.
If any provision of this Act, or the application thereof, is held invalid, the validity of the remainder of this Act.
"Public Law 7-47 | The Federal Bar Association Act of 2022" is hereby repealed.
There is established in the Executive branch an Association to be known as the Federal Bar Association.
The Federal Bar Examination shall be an exam that is created and administered to citizens by the Chairman.
provisions.notes: Administrative functions and enforcement rules belong in Title 5.

current_implementation.detail: The draft inserted a boilerplate note at a Title 50 anchor with a full-text dump and Trello link, but the act needs real Title 5 codification for the new association and its governing provisions.

source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-012-074/law.txt contains the establishment and governance text for the Federal Bar Association.
audit/primary/batch-12-02.json classifies the act as current and amendment-required.
audit/claude-validation.json agrees that the branch only has a note-only implementation.
Current XML grep did not recover a matching PL-012-074 note id.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current XML search did not recover the branch note or the Title 5 establishment text; origin/main likewise does not show a better placement.
laws.final_recommended_action: Move sections 4 and 5 into Title 5 as new organic text, keep sections 1 to 3 as note or history material, and delete the boilerplate Title 50 note.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology supports the current branch treatment and the law remains aligned with the documented codification path.

laws.title: Public Law 12-75 | Freedom of Information Common Sense Act

provisions.evidence: This act shall be referred to as the Freedom of Information Common Sense Act.
It is the purpose of this act to lower the response time limit for all Freedom of Information Act Requests.
5 U.S. Code section 552(a)(6)(A)(i), (ii), and (ii)(I) shall be replaced with 4-day deadline language.
provisions.notes: This is a live FOIA amendment.

current_implementation.detail: The draft note is parked in Title 44 with a full-text dump and Trello link, but the real target is 5 U.S.C. section 552.

source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-012-075/law.txt contains the FOIA deadline replacements in section 3.
audit/primary/batch-12-02.json classifies the law as current and a live Code amendment.
audit/claude-validation.json also classifies the law as current and amendment-required.
Current XML grep did not recover a matching PL-012-075 note id.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch XML search did not recover a matching FOIA note, and origin/main does not show a better placement.
laws.final_recommended_action: Apply the FOIA deadline replacements in 5 U.S.C. 552 and reduce the retained material to a concise statutory note.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology supports the current branch treatment and the law remains aligned with the documented codification path.

laws.title: Public Law 12-81 | The Integrity Act

provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the The Integrity Act.
If any provision of this Act, or the application thereof, is held invalid, the validity of the remainder of this Act.
All, if any previous laws that conflict with this Act shall be repealed.
Whereas, in the past, Presidents have removed us from organizations like the United Nations with no congressional notice or consultation.
The President of the United States SHALL NOT have the authority to remove the United States from any treaty, organizational group, or any foreign group without the consultation of the senate.

current_implementation.detail: The current review record places the note in Title 22, but the canonical anchor is 2 U.S.C. 6567.

source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-012-081/law.txt contains the general consultation rule and repeal/conflict clause.
audit/primary/batch-12-02.json classifies the law as current and note-only-but-amendment-required.
audit/claude-validation.json classifies the law as current and misanchored-note.
Current XML grep did not recover a matching PL-012-081 note id.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch XML search did not recover a matching note identifier, and origin/main does not show a better anchor; the canonical destination remains 2 U.S.C. 6567.
laws.final_recommended_action: Move the note to 2 U.S.C. 6567 and keep the treatment as a statutory note.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology supports the current branch treatment and the law remains aligned with the documented codification path.
